questions.question: Explain the backpropagation algorithm in a multilayer feedforward neural network, including the chain rule derivation for updating weights in the hidden layer.
keyPoints: Chain rule application for computing gradients from output to input layer
Recursive gradient computation: δl = (W(l+1)T δ(l+1)) ⊙ σ'(zl)

questions.question: Discuss the impact of key hyperparameters—learning rate, momentum, number of hidden layers, and batch size—on the training performance of multilayer feedforward networks.
keyPoints: Learning rate: too high causes divergence, too slow convergence
Momentum: accelerates convergence by dampening oscillations
Hidden layers: more layers enable complex representations but increase computational cost
Batch size: affects gradient variance and memory requirements

questions.question: Derive the complete mathematical formulation of a multilayer feedforward network with one hidden layer, including: (a) forward propagation equations with activation functions, (b) loss function selection (cross-entropy and MSE), and (c) backpropagation updates for output and hidden layer weights using gradient descent.
keyPoints: Forward: z(1) = W(1)x + b(1), a(1) = σ(z(1)), z(2) = W(2)a(1) + b(2), ŷ = softmax(z(2)) for classification
Loss functions: L = -∑y log(ŷ) for cross-entropy, L = 1/2∑(y-ŷ)² for MSE
Output layer gradients: ∂L/∂W(2) = δ(2)a(1)T where δ(2) = (ŷ-y) ⊙ σ'(z(2))
Hidden layer gradients: δ(1) = (W(2)Tδ(2)) ⊙ σ'(z(1)), weight update: W ← W - η∂L/∂W

questions.question: Compare gradient descent (GD), stochastic gradient descent (SGD), and mini-batch gradient descent for training multilayer feedforward networks. Include mathematical formulation, convergence behavior, and practical implementation considerations with pseudo-code or code snippets in Python/PyTorch.
keyPoints: GD: θ = θ - η∇θL(θ) using full dataset, smooth convergence but computationally expensive
SGD: θ = θ - η∇θL(θ;xi,yi) using one sample, noisy but faster per iteration, escapes local minima
Mini-batch: θ = θ - η∇θL(θ;B) using B samples, balances both advantages, standard practice (batch_size=32-256)
Implementation: DataLoader usage, learning rate scheduling, early stopping, and vanishing gradient solutions (ReLU, batch normalization)
